old_claim.note: Rifling in Firearms provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Rifled cannon and other post-Civil-War weapon innovations created the need for a dedicated proving ground where new ordnance could be tested.

source_shape.source_locator: NPS says rifled cannon fired pointed-nosed projectiles farther and faster after the Civil War, and the Army needed a place to test its own new weapons.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source identifies rifled-cannon innovation as a direct reason for establishing the Sandy Hook proving ground.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports rifling_firearms as an enabling edge because the proving ground's source-backed purpose was testing new rifled ordnance and related weapons.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved and strengthened: industrial_ballistics_laboratories keeps rifling_firearms as a direct dependency, now source-backed.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped away from ordnance proving grounds to chemical propellant laboratories only.
A stronger source showed Sandy Hook was not created to test rifled or post-Civil-War ordnance.
The rifling_firearms node were narrowed away from rifled artillery and firearm barrel technology.
short_reason: Rifled ordnance was a source-backed driver for the proving ground.
